name: Milan
body:
Milan in v3 is no longer the arrival decompression — Day 1 drives MXP straight to Alba. Instead Milan does the trip's farewell work as a continuous two-night Brera stay. The Tue Jun 2 afternoon arrival from Liguria drops bags at Antica Locanda Solferino on Via Castelfidardo, an eleven-room family-run pension operating since 1926, four minutes' walk from the Pinacoteca di Brera. The early evening unfolds at full energy: Camparino in Galleria for a Campari Soda from the 1932 column tap, a Quadrilatero pass-through, then the 15-minute taxi to Diego Rossi's Trattoria Trippa in Porta Romana — trippa alla parmigiana, vitello tonnato, the offal-forward kitchen the brief asks for.

Wednesday Jun 3 is the full Milan day. Quadrilatero d'Oro window-and-shop loop in the morning, lunch at Ratanà or a quieter Brera trattoria, the Pinacoteca Ambrosiana for the Caravaggio-and-Codex-Atlanticus afternoon, then a slow walking loop through Brera's antique streets before dinner. Brooks picks the dinner register based on Tuesday's Trippa intensity — quiet Brera trattoria, Navigli osteria, or Cracco at the Galleria for the anniversary anchor if that is the night Brooks reserves for it.

anchors.name: Camparino in Galleria
anchors.blurb: 1915 bar in the Galleria, original Campari column tap from 1932

anchors.name: Galleria Vittorio Emanuele II
anchors.blurb: Mengoni's 1877 iron-and-glass arcade, the crossroads between Duomo and La Scala

anchors.name: Quadrilatero d'Oro
anchors.blurb: Via Monte Napoleone / Via della Spiga / Via Sant'Andrea — the window-walk register, not the shopping list

anchors.name: Brera district
anchors.blurb: Sant'Ambrogio-era streets converted into the post-war artists' quarter; the right walking texture for a 20:00 dinner approach
